machine_note: Maker Nexus / home shop CNC for walnut frame routing only. Bar work is bench-top: chop saw + drill press + bench file. Verify exact CNC machine, spindle, collet, bed size, and hold-down before CAM.
assumptions: This is a pre-CAM operation graph, not verified G-code.
The active build range is C5-C7 chromatic, 25 bars (with 10-bar pentatonic art-fair variant sharing stock).
Source dimensions come from family-spec.csv and glockenspiel-design-table.xlsx.
Bars are flat 6061-T6 aluminum stock (0.250 in × 1.000 in). NO arch undercut, NO resonator tubes (per design.md).
Walnut frame is the only true CNC operation. Bar work is bench-top.
Effective K constant is calibrated from C5/A5/C7 pilot measurements; workbook K = 204431 is a first-pass.

release_criteria: C5 cut-long Hz produces K_eff within 5% of workbook K, OR family-spec.csv updated with calibrated K.
A5 trims to within +/- 5 cents of 880 Hz; mounting does not damp below 1.5 s sustain.
C7 trim discipline lands within +/- 10 cents in 0.05 in length increments.
Mounting prototype rig (JD-060) confirms grommet durometer choice.
Frame layout template (JD-070) confirms ergonomic reach.

operations.name: Review design package and pilot gate
operations.datum: Top face A, left end B, bar centerline C
checks: Full-set cutting held until pilot data reviewed
operations.notes: Do not cut full set until pilot bars validate K constant and trim discipline.

operations.name: Receive and verify aluminum stock
checks: No deep scratches in playing area
Mill lot recorded for K-calibration traceability
operations.notes: Different mill lots can shift K by 5-10%; record lot ID in validation.csv.

operations.name: Mark bar length and node positions
operations.tool: Stop-block sled, square, fine marker, centerline gauge
operations.notes: Cut-long discipline: every bar marked at predicted length plus 0.25 in trim allowance.

operations.name: Cut bars to length
checks: Bar labeled with member_id and target Hz
operations.notes: Deburr immediately after cutting; aluminum burrs are easy to miss and cause node-hole tear-out.

operations.name: Drill node holes
operations.tool: 3/16 in HSS or carbide bit, hardwood backer, depth stop
operations.workholding: V-block jig (JD-040) clamped to drill-press table
operations.datum: Left end B against jig fence, centerline C
outputs: Bars with two clean node holes
operations.notes: Peck-drill, lubricate with cutting fluid, inspect each hole before moving to next bar.

operations.name: Strike-test on foam supports (flat_bar)
operations.tool: Foam blocks at node positions, chromatic tuner, microphone
outputs: Measured Hz, sustain, timbre per pilot bar
checks: K_eff = f * L^2 / t computed for each pilot
Sustain to first 6 dB drop logged
operations.notes: Pilot only (C5/A5/C7) before drilling node holes is unnecessary because length isn't yet trimmed; this row records the post-drill flat-bar measurement.

operations.name: Trim to pitch (post_trim)
operations.machine: Bench / belt sander or fine file
operations.tool: Belt sander or bench file, calipers, tuner
outputs: Bars trimmed to within +/- 10 cents (pilots), +/- 5 cents (production)
checks: Material removed symmetrically from both ends
operations.notes: Sneak up slowly. Raising pitch is hard to reverse.

operations.name: Walnut frame: receive and inspect
operations.notes: Hard maple or cherry acceptable substitutes; frame wood does not affect bar tuning.

operations.name: Walnut frame: CNC fixture and datum verification
operations.tool: Spoilboard surfacing bit, edge finder/probe, clamps/tape
operations.workholding: Replaceable spoilboard, datum fence, optional vacuum
operations.datum: Machine X0 at left rail end, Y0 at rail centerline, Z0 top face

operations.name: Walnut frame: rough rail profile and grommet pockets
operations.tool: 1/4 in downcut spiral; 1/8 in upcut for pocket details
outputs: Profiled walnut rails with grommet pockets
checks: Pocket dia and depth match design
operations.notes: Front rail (15 naturals or 7 pentatonic naturals) and back rail (10 sharps or 3 pentatonic sharps) cut separately.

operations.name: Walnut frame: optional inlay engraving
operations.tool: 1/8 in V-bit (60 deg) for V-carve, OR diode laser for surface engraving
operations.notes: Per workbook row 115: Broinwood-style inlay or laser-engraved logo on rail sides. Skip for pilot build.

operations.name: Walnut frame: drill stainless rod through-holes and fastener holes
operations.tool: 1/8 in (rod hole), pilot bits for fastener holes
operations.workholding: End-cap fixture or rail end fixture
checks: End-cap holes align with rail holes
operations.notes: Run a slip-fit test with the actual stainless rod before assembly.

operations.name: Mounting prototype rig (durometer pilot)
operations.tool: Three short walnut blocks with grommet pockets, three durometer grommets, paracord, 1/8 in stainless rod
outputs: Sustain and pitch-shift data per durometer
operations.notes: Pilot Shore 30A, 50A, 70A on C5/A5/C7 bars before standardizing on walnut frame.

operations.name: Frame assembly and bar mounting
checks: Cord captures every bar at both nodes
operations.notes: Use removable fasteners until tuning and buzz checks are done.

operations.name: Final validation and release
operations.tool: Chromatic tuner, microphone, calipers, mallets (hard rubber + brass), camera
operations.datum: Same support and strike datum used for every note
checks: Every framed bar within +/- 5 cents at soft dynamic
No persistent buzz at any dynamic
Sustain consistent across row to within 30%
operations.notes: Do not declare the build complete until measured data closes the loop.

release_checks: Every operation has a datum and workholding method.
Every tool has a real machine available or an escalation note.
All tuning-critical features include trim allowance.
C5/A5/C7 pilot data is reviewed before cutting all 25 bars.
Grommet durometer is selected from pilot data, not assumed.
validation.csv receives measured data after the first prototype.
K_eff is updated in family-spec.csv if it differs from workbook K by more than 5%.
